popularStory.headline: Hugh de Kevelioc, 5th Earl of Chester
popularStory.description:
Hugh de Kevelioc, 5th Earl of Chester

Hugh de Kevelioc, 5th Earl of Chester (1147 – 30 June 1181) was the son of Ranulf de Gernon, 4th Earl of Chester and Maud of Gloucester, daughter of Robert, 1st …

zh.St Werburghs, Chester, Cheshire, England: 英國英格蘭爾切斯特聖 維包也斯 邱奇
zh.lifeSummary: 當Hugh of Cyfeiliog 5th Earl of Chester於大約1147年出生在英國威爾斯波斯時，他的父親Ranulf II de Gernon48歲，母親Maud of Gloucester29歲。他於1169年與Bertrade de Montfort結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和4個女兒的父母。他去世於1181年6月30日在英國斯塔福爾利可享年35歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭爾切斯特聖 維包也斯 邱奇。

zh-hans.St Werburghs, Chester, Cheshire, England: 英国英格兰爾切斯特聖 維包也斯 邱奇
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Hugh of Cyfeiliog 5th Earl of Chester于大约1147年出生在英国威尔斯波斯时，他的父亲Ranulf II de Gernon48岁，母亲Maud of Gloucester29岁。他于1169年与Bertrade de Montfort结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和4个女儿的父母。他去世于1181年6月30日在英国斯塔福爾利可享年35岁，埋葬在英国英格兰爾切斯特聖 維包也斯 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Cheng, Jing, Kam Fung, Wai Shing, Wing, Yali, Yuk.
variant Romanization of the surnames 歐 and 區, see   Ou   1 and 2.

variants.name: Ou
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ying, Ching, Heng, Hong, Jia, Kuang Yu, Li-Ching, Cho, Chong, Chung, Sa.  Southeast Asian Lichun, Ming, Quan, Roeun, Sokhom, Chau, Hai, Leang, Ly, Nhanh, Phan, Pho, Tha.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 歐, meaning ‘to sing someone's praise’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from the placename Dong Ou (東甌), a fief (in present-day Wenzhou in Zhejiang province) which was granted to Yao in 192  BC . It is said that Yao was a descendant of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ). (ii) from the placename Ou Yu (歐餘), the name of a fief (located in present-day Zhejiang province) that was granted to Ti, the second son of Wujiang (reigned 355–333  BC ), king of the state of Yue (located mainly in present-day Zhejiang and Jiangxi provinces) during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ).

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 區, originally denoting an ancient vessel for measurement in ancient Chinese: from the personal name of Ou Zhizi (歐治子), an expert sword-maker who lived during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476 BC ). Some of his descendants inherited Ou (歐) as their surname, simplifying the Chinese character 歐 to 區.

nameMeanings.name: Hugh
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French name, Hugues, Hugo, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from hug ‘heart, mind, spirit’. It was originally a short form of various compound names containing this element. This was borne by the aristocracy of medieval France, adopted by the Normans, and introduced by them to Britain. It was consistently one of the most popular boys' names in the Middle Ages in England, but fell out of favour in the 16th century until it was revived in the 20th century. Little Hugh of Lincoln was a child supposed in the Middle Ages to have been murdered by Jews in about 1255 , a legend responsible for several outbursts of anti-Semitism at various times. The story is referred to by Chaucer in The Prioress's Tale. He is not to be confused with St Hugh of Lincoln ( 1140–1200 ), bishop of Lincoln ( 1186–1200 ), who was noted for his charity and good works, his piety, and his defence of the Church against the State. Since the later Middle Ages Hugh has also been used as an Anglicized form of the Gaelic name Aodh .

en.lifeSummary: When Hugh of Cyfeiliog 5th Earl of Chester was born about 1147, in Powys, Wales, United Kingdom, his father, Ranulf II de Gernon, was 48 and his mother, Maud of Gloucester, was 29. He married Bertrade de Montfort in 1169. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 4 daughters. He died on 30 June 1181, in Leek, Staffordshire, England, at the age of 35, and was buried in St Werburgh's Church, Chester, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.St Werburghs, Chester, Cheshire, England: St Werburgh's Church, Chester, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Hugh of Cyfeiliog 5th Earl of Chester est né environ 1147, à Powys, Pays de Galles, Royaume-Uni, son père, Ranulf II de Gernon, avait 48 ans et sa mère, Maud of Gloucester, avait 29 ans. Il a épousé Bertrade de Montfort en 1169. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 4 filles. Il est décédé le 30 juin 1181, à Leek, Staffordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 35 ans, et a été enterré à St Werburgh's Church, Chester, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.St Werburghs, Chester, Cheshire, England: St Werburgh's Church, Chester, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom
de.lifeSummary: Als Hugh of Cyfeiliog 5th Earl of Chester um 1147 in Powys, Wales, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Ranulf II de Gernon 48 und seine Mutter Maud of Gloucester 29. Er hat 1169 Bertrade de Montfort geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 4 Töchter. Er starb am 30. Juni 1181 im Alter von 35 Jahren in Leek, Staffordshire, England und wurde in St Werburgh's Church, Chester, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Hugh of Cyfeiliog 5th Earl of Chester è nato il circa 1147 nel Powys, Galles, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Ranulf II de Gernon, aveva 48 anni e il suo madre, Maud of Gloucester, aveva 29 anni. Lui sposato Bertrade de Montfort nel 1169. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 4 figlie. Morì il 30 giugno 1181, nel Leek, Staffordshire, England, all'età di 35 anni, e fu sepolto a St Werburgh's Church, Chester, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ko.St Werburghs, Chester, Cheshire, England: 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 체스터 스트 워버그h'스 처르치
ko.lifeSummary: Hugh of Cyfeiliog 5th Earl of Chester(이)가 대략 1147년 영국 웨일스 파우이스에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Ranulf II de Gernon(은)는 48세였고, 그의 어머니 Maud of Gloucester(은)는 29세였다. 그는 1169년 Bertrade de Montfort(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1181년 6월 30일 영국 스태퍼드실르 리크에서 35세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 체스터 스트 워버그h'스 처르치에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Hugh of Cyfeiliog 5th Earl of Chester nasceu aproximadamente 1147, em Powys, Gales, United Kingdom, seu pai, Ranulf II de Gernon, tinha 48 anos e sua mãe, Maud of Gloucester, tinha 29 anos. Ele casou-se com Bertrade de Montfort em 1169. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 4 filhas. Ele faleceu em 30 de junho de 1181, em Leek, Staffordshire, Inglaterra, com 35 anos, e foi sepultado em St Werburgh's Church, Chester, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.St Werburghs, Chester, Cheshire, England: ст вирбург'с чурч, честир, чешир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Hugh of Cyfeiliog 5th Earl of Chester родился около 1147 г. в местности поуис, Уэльс, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Ranulf II de Gernon было 48 лет, а матери, которую звали Maud of Gloucester, 29. Он женился на женщине по имени Bertrade de Montfort в 1169 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 4 дочери. Он умер 30 июня 1181 г. в местности лик, стаффордшир, Англия в возрасте 35 лет и был похоронен в местности ст вирбург'с чурч, честир, чешир, Англия, Великобритания.
